pages.content:
HTTPS AND TLS

Friday, 10 November, 2023

07:09 AM

HOW TO: Configure HTTPs in Informatica Administrator Console with a self-signed certificate

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/121092?language=en_US

HOW TO: Generate custom self-signed certificates and enable TLS for domain/node ?

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000206096?language=en_US

HOW TO: Disable HTTPS (SSL connectivity) for Admin console in the Domain

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/512183?language=en_US

HOW TO: Create a Keystore file from privatekey, certificate, and certificate chain and enable HTTPS to Admin Console using this Keystore file in PC

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000183570?language=en_US

HOW TO: Configure HTTPS for Administrator Console in PowerCenter using secure certificates provided by Certification Authorities such as Verisign

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/111527?language=en_US

KB articles for refrense.

FAQ: Which versions of Java are bundled with Informatica PowerCenter?

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/519956

HOW TO: Create custom Keystores and Truststores to be configured with PowerCenter

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/221149

HOW TO: Configure HTTPs in Informatica Administrator Console with a self-signed certificate

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/121092

Steps to enable the HTTPS with self signed sertificate

need to create a keystore file

step 1: -

keytool -genkey -dname "CN=, OU=GCS, O=infa, L=Bangalore, S=Karnataka, C=India" -alias noor -keypass infa@123 -keystore noorkeystore.jks -storepass infa@123 -keyalg "RSA" -keysize 2048 -validity 1000 -ext san=dns:

step 2:

list the content there in the keystore

keytool -list -v -keystore noorkeystore.jks

step 3:

stop the services

take the backup of nodemeta.xml from isp/config

step 4: update the gateway node

infa_home/isp/bin/ ./infasetup.sh updategatewaynode -hs 8443 -kf tomcat/config/noorkeystore.jks -kp infa@123

step 5: verifiy to access the adminconsole on HTTPS port and verify the keystore location for the node.

step 6:

after you access the adminconsole you still see the nosecure message because our browser is still not trusting the certificat.

>export the certificat from the browser and then install the certificat to the current user and place it in a trusted root location .

YouTube videos for reference

﷟HYPERLINK "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JU6TB67FTKE&ab_channel=InformaticaSupport"Part 1- TLS in PowerCenter

﷟HYPERLINK "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v6i_RIIM0a4&ab_channel=InformaticaSupport"Part 2: How to Enable and Disable HTTPS and Node TLS using Self-signed Certificates

﷟HYPERLINK "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xR4GhvTy7WU&t=30s&ab_channel=InformaticaSupport"Part 3 - How to Enable HTTPS for the Admin Console URL using CA Signed Certificates in PowerCenter

﷟HYPERLINK "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0gV8NeAST6I&ab_channel=InformaticaSupport"PART 4 - How to Enable Node-level TLS using CA-signed Certificates in PowerCenter

HOW TO: Generate and configure Custom keystore and truststore for Informatica Services to enable secure communication using the certificates provided by the CA (Certification Authority) in PowerCenter

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/511374?language=en_US

How to enable TLS and HTTPS using the CA signed Certificates

1. Create your private key

keytool -genkey -alias infa -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 1000 -keystore vento_infa_keystore.jks -ext san=dns:vento.informatica.com,dns:inglx78.informatica.com

2. Create the Certificate Signing Request ( CSR ) and submit the CSR to a Certification Authority (CA)

keytool -certreq -keyalg RSA -alias infa -file infa.csr -keystore vento_infa_keystore.jks -ext san=dns:vento.informatica.com,dns:inglx78.informatica.com

need to send infa.csr to CA-signed certificate.

3.Once we get back the certificate from CA, import the site/replay certificate, root, intermidiate key to infa_keystoe.jks

keytool -import -alias root -keystore infa_keystore.jks -trustcacerts -file root.cer

keytool -import -alias intermed -keystore infa_keystore.jks -trustcacerts -file intermidiate.cer

keytool -import -alias infa -keystore infa_keystore.jks -trustcacerts -file site.cer

4.Deleting the root and intermediate certificates from infa_keystore.jks file as the infa.cer had the chain of certificates :

keytool -delete -noprompt -alias root -keystore infa_keystore.jks -storepass changeit

keytool -delete -noprompt -alias intermed -keystore infa_keystore.jks -storepass changeit

NOTE: Without importing the above certificates, import of infa.cer in infa_keystore.jks file failed with error : "Keytool error: java.lang.Exception: Failed to establish chain from reply"

5.Creating infa_keystore.pem file :

keytool -importkeystore -srcstoretype JKS -srckeystore infa_keystore.jks -deststoretype PKCS12 -destkeystore infa_keystore.pkcs12

openssl pkcs12 -in infa_keystore.pkcs12 -nodes -out infa_keystore.pem

6.Creating and importing certificates to infa_truststore.jks file :

keytool -import -alias root -keystore infa_truststore.jks -trustcacerts -file root.cer

keytool -import -alias intermed -keystore infa_truststore.jks -trustcacerts -file intermidiate.cer

keytool -import -alias infa -keystore infa_truststore.jks -trustcacerts -file site.cer

7.Creating infa_truststore.pem file:

keytool -importkeystore -srcstoretype JKS -srckeystore infa_truststore.jks -deststoretype PKCS12 -destkeystore infa_truststore.pkcs12

openssl pkcs12 -in infa_truststore.pkcs12 -nodes -out infa_truststore.pem

8.Enabling https in admin console level

./infasetup.sh updateGatewayNode -HttpsPort 24478 -KeystoreFile /home/amarkumar/custom_vento/infa_keystore.jks -KeystorePass changeit

9.For enabling the tls in the node level

./infasetup.sh UpdateGatewayNode -tls TRUE -nk /home/amarkumar/custom_vento -nkp changeit -nt /home/amarkumar/custom_vento -ntp changeit

HOW TO: What are the steps taken for an SSL domain certificate renewal for Informatica

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/535316?language=en_US

04763145 SSL Handshake Exception Error

HOW TO CONFIGURE HTTPS USING THE CA CERTIFICATE

Step 1: Generate the Keystore

Run the following command to generate the keystore:

keytool -genkey -dname "CN=host.informatica.com, OU=GCS, O=informatica, L=Bangalore, S=Karnataka, C=India" -alias tomcat -keypass infa@123 -keystore tomcat.keystore -storepass infa@123 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 365 -ext san=dns:host1,dns:host2,ip:ip_address1,ip:ip_address2

Step 2: Generate the Certificate Signing Request (CSR)

Use the same alias as in Step 1:

keytool -certreq -keyalg RSA -alias tomcat -file infa.csr -keystore tomcat.keystore -ext san=dns:host1,dns:host2,ip:ip_address1,ip:ip_address2

Note: The alias used here must match the one used in Step 1.

Step 3: Import Certificates Received from the CA

Import the Root Certificate: keytool -import -alias root -keystore tomcat.keystore -trustcacerts -file root.crt

Import the Intermediate Certificate: keytool -import -alias intermed -keystore tomcat.keystore -trustcacerts -file intermediate.crt

Import the Signed Certificate (Site/Reply Certificate): Use the same alias as in Step 1 and 2: keytool -import -alias tomcat -keystore tomcat.keystore -trustcacerts -file reply.crt

Important: This step replaces the temporary self-signed certificate generated during CSR creation.

Step 4: Update the Gateway Node Configuration

Navigate to the configuration directory: cd $INFA_HOME/isp/config

Backup the existing nodemeta.xml file.

Run the following command to update the gateway node: cd $INFA_HOME/isp/bin

./infasetup updateGatewayNode -hs 8443 -kf /tomcat.keystore -kp infa@123

Step 5: Perform Tomcat Cleanup

Follow this KB: ﷟HYPERLINK "https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/501418"HOW TO: Perform Tomcat cleanup for Informatica Services

Default keystore password - xer@vSTp(tKwc#*3156@n!x

Default truststore password - ﷟HYPERLINK "mailto:pass2038@infaSSL"pass2038@infaSSL

Default location $INFA_HOME/services/shared/security

pages.title: Logs & Debugging
pages.content:
Logs & Debugging

Friday, June 7, 2024

5:07 AM

PowerCenter Troubleshooting and Log collection Guide

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/571936

PowerCenter issues can be broadly classified into three types:

1. Hang

2. Crash

3. Failure

4. Performance

5. High CPU

6. High Memory

Refer https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/17588?language=en_US

Beforewe get to the individual problem types, we need to be sure to collect RepositoryService, Integration Service, domain, and INFA_HOME\logsfor all the above scenarios. These are mandatory fortroubleshooting any of the below issues.

Hang Process analysis diagnostics

What is a stack?

Stack gives the process snapshot at a given instant of time. It shows what are the functions being executed by the process at the time of the collection of the stack.

When a process is hung, we need the stacks on the process, as well as its parent processes and the processes to which it communicates to.

So, for PC services, when the IS/RS/domain is hung, we need to collect stacks on all the three processes.

When IS is on grid, collecting stacks on both master and non-master IS is essential. If the domain has issues, we need to collect jstack on all nodes in the domain including worker nodes.

When the client is hung, collect the dumps on the client tools and Repository/ Integration service.

Note

In order to understand whether the process is hung, we need snapshots of the process at various time intervals. Hence collecting 1 stack alone will not yield us any information. Collect multiple stacks at certain intervals ranging from 30 seconds to1 minute interval based on the issue.

Linux Commands

1.pmstack -p

2.jstack

Windows

Right click on the process in the task manager, and then do create a full user dump

Crashanalysis diagnostics

When the application crashes, based on the process type java/native (C++), the process creates a core dump/Java heapdump by default in Linux and in Windows, the core dumps won't be generated.

If the dumps are not created by the process, review the respective parent process logs.

Example

For RS crash review the domain logs, for IS crash review the domain logs during the time of the issue. If the dumps are not available in Linux, please validate their ulimites and see if the core file limit is set using the command:

cat /proc//limits

Generate the crash dumps on Windows

So, the crash in windows cannot be identified at the very first occurrence.

We need to check the event viewer logs for the same KB 423971

If the event viewer logs are not yielding any information, we need to configure the debug diag and wait for the next occurrence.

KB:123835

For the core dumps on Linux:

Collect the trace files on the core dumps :

pmstack -c

Libscollector output KB:144522

If more troubleshooting is needed to identify the memory corruptions, we need the libscollector.shoutput on the core, this is needed from the customer to find the memory corruptions, libscollector.shbinds all the necessary libraries which a process is using to a single zip. This has to be analyzed internally and then we can isolate the memory corruptions.

For the Informatica client crashes, follow the Windows crash process and then capture the required dumps.

Failure analysisdiagnostics

Failures are unique, they donot fall under any of the above categories. This meansthe process was gracefully shutdown. Which means, the application had handled the failure and it knows why it failed.

This issue does not require many logs to troubleshoot as the first level. Collect only the respective process and its parent process logs.

Performance analysis diagnostics

We can actually see performance issues as the hang to greater extent, just that in case of performance the threads are moving slow, whereas in hang, the threads are not moving at all.

We would need stacks from on the process during the time, when the delay is seen. Stacks taken before and after the issue will not help.

Collect the process text logs, for the run, where the delay is not seen and when the delay is seen. This will help us to weigh the performance degradation.

High CPU consumption analysis diagnostics

When a process consumes high CPU, the first analysis should be to isolate and find the function which in code, contributes to the high CPU. This can be done only by collecting the CPU usage of the process along with the percentage of the CPU consumed by each process.

Thus, to conclude, to troubleshoot high CPU, the most important diagnostics, is the thread level CPU consumption and the stack traces collected during the time of the issue. Identify the top CPU consuming process, collect their logs and the thread level top using the below steps.

Windows:KB:514201

Linux: KB:314424

When all the above logs are collected and analyzed, we will now know, where the problem is and it can be further classified as the following sub-problems:

System Load issue

Validate ulimits on their system.

cat /proc//limits

When the system is overloaded and if the suspicion is on this front, collect the SAR report from Linux admins, performance monitor output in Windowsand GC logging for the node Java process.

File System Activity issue

If the troubleshooting has to go more advanced and we suspect the issue is related to file system, we will need system level file system activities traced.

Windows

procmon output KB:154250

Linux

strace -ttT -fF -s 65535 -p pid -o out.txt

lsof -p >lsof.out

netstat -peano>netstat.out

Once strace is captured, in-order to trace the file descriptor ids, we need the lsof and netstatoutput on the same process in linux, hence collect thoseoutputs as well. With Windows, we get this from procmon directly, hence there are no new separate commands needed.

Network issue

When we isolated the issue to be network, from the above stacktraces and errors, we need to trace the packets across the two machines which are involved in the delay. Network capture should be our last resort in any of our troubleshooting methodologies. This will generate a lot of dumps for the users and hence, it is ideally not the resort to start the troubleshooting.

The packets have to be captured both at the source and target, this is essential, to see if the packets actually arrived at the other end point or not. Hence when tcpdumps are collected, these have to be collected at both the end points which are involved in the communication.

Windows

Capture the wireshark KB:560905

Linux

Capture TCPdumps: KB:509347

pages.title: ADMIN CONSOLE - DEBUG
pages.content:
ADMIN CONSOLE - DEBUG

Friday, 29 September, 2023

07:27 AM

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/334583?language=en_US

================================================================================================

04652691 - Admin console crash and unexplained behavior

-need to check the catalina.out for errors and increase the java heap space

"java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Metaspace" when Adminconsole process is down in PC

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/Adminconsole-process-is-down-with-error-java-lang-OutOfMemoryError-Metaspace?language=en_US

================================================================================================

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/How-to-debug-admin-console-issues?language=en_US

Step 1

We first need to confirm whether or not the Admin console PID was started successfully.

To do this, go to$INFA_HOME\tomcat\temp\_AdminConsole\logsdirectory and check the last line of the latest catalina.log file. You should see a message as shown below:

INFO: Server startup in 27107 ms

Then let us check the node.log.

This line in the node.log file shows that the admin console is started:

[Thread 6 of 6 in DomainServiceThreadPool] [SPC_10018] Request to start process for service [_AdminConsole] was successful.

================================================================================================

Step 2

Ensure that the Administrator Console Java process is up and running fine. Look for Java process running from$INFA_HOME\java\bindirectory with thekeyword _AdminConsoleor AdministratorConsole in the command line.

On Windows, you could check it from theTask Manager > Processestab. If the Command-Line column is not there, you will need to add it fromMenu Bar > View > Select Columns. On UNIX (non-Windows) platform, you could check it using thepscommand as shown below:

ps -ef | grep | grep _AdminConsole

Example

ps -ef | grep InfaUser | grep _AdminConsole

================================================================================================

Step 3

The admin console prints messages in the following logs:

node.log, catalina.out, node.jsf

================================================================================================

Step 4

If the Admin console, Java PID is started then chances are there is a firewall that is blocking the network connection to it.

In order to confirm this theory you would need to do the following:

First, ping the domain from the Linux box:

﷟HYPERLINK "http://infacmd.sh/"infacmd.sh ping -dn Domain_multigatewaynode

(replaceDomain_multigatewaynodewith your actual domain name)

Then, ping the domain from client tools.

In order for theinfacmd pingto go to the right server, it uses the domains.infa file. Ensure that this environment variable is set.

INFA_DOMAINS_FILE=${INFA_HOME}/domains.infa

infacmd.bat ping -dn Domain_multigatewaynode -hp my_server:16005

(replaceDomain_multigatewaynodewith your actual domain name)

(replacemy_server:16005with your actual hostname and port number for the main node)

If the ping is ok from the server and not from the client tool then this is an indication of a network issue.

================================================================================================

Step 5

If the admin console is not starting.

If you don't see the "Request to start process for service [_AdminConsole] was successful" message in the node log.

Then, try to enable the Administration Console service through the command line,use theinfacmd isp enableservicecommand as follows:

infacmd isp enableservice -dn -un -pd -sdn Native -hp -sn _adminconsole

Then, we can try to redeploy the admin console app in tomcat.

Do the following:

Shutdown the PC domain by running ﷟HYPERLINK "http://infaservice.sh/"infaservice.sh shutdown or by clicking Administrator Console > Actions > Shutdown Domain OR you can do the same from the command line.

On UNIX, run the following:

﷟HYPERLINK "http://infaservice.sh/"infaservice.sh shutdown

Then, Cleanup Tomcat cache by running the following commands:

mkdir $INFA_HOME/tmp

mkdir $INFA_HOME/tmp/localhost

mkdir $INFA_HOME/tmp/temp

mkdir $INFA_HOME/tmp/webapps_adminconsole

mkdir $INFA_HOME/tmp/AdministratorConsole_adminconsole

mv $INFA_HOME/tomcat/work/Catalina/localhost/* $INFA_HOME/tmp/localhost

mv $INFA_HOME/tomcat/temp/* $INFA_HOME/tmp/temp

mv $INFA_HOME/tomcat/webapps/adminconsole/* $INFA_HOME/tmp/webapps_adminconsole

mv $INFA_HOME/services/AdministratorConsole/adminconsole/* $INFA_HOME/tmp/AdministratorConsole_adminconsole

Note

It is recommended to take a backup.

The command above is for illustration purposes only.

If these commands delete or move the .war files. You would need to put them back in the original directory.

Please do not remove *.war files from any directory since the *.war files are used in order to redeploy the application

Then restart the PC services.

﷟HYPERLINK "http://infaservice.sh/"infaservice.sh startup

Then check if the Administrator Console is launched. You should get the following message in the node.log file:

[DOM_10067] Core Service [LogServiceAgent] has been successfully enabled.

[Thread 2 of 6 in DomainServiceThreadPool] [SPC_10018] Request to start process for service [_AdminConsole]

Executing the steps above will redeploy the AdminConsole application from *.war file in the Tomcat Application Service.

Do not remove *.war files from any directory as the *.war files will be used to redeploy the application.

For your reference, here are some steps that you can perform in order to start the admin console manually

use theinfacmd EnableServicecommand:

================================================================================================

For example,

To disable the Administration Console service through the command line, use theinfacmd isp disableservicecommand:

infacmd isp disableservice -dn -un -pd -sdn Native -hp -sn _adminconsole -mo Stop

To enable the Administration Console service through the command line, use theinfacmd isp enableservicecommand:

infacmd isp enableservice -dn -un -pd -sdn Native -hp -sn _adminconsole

================================================================================================

=======================================

Admin console Network related issues

Run the following From server / client

Ping

Telnet

Curl -I http://hostname:6005 - this should return the html

=======================================

ERROR: "FATAL [Domain Monitor] [DOM_10094] Cannot update the data for the master gateway node within the refresh interval time [32000]. The node will not continue as a master gateway node" in node.log file when the Informatica Domain fails

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/145762?language=en_US

Please set MasterDBRefreshInterval custom parameter to 30. Following is how to set it:

- Login to the Administration Console

- Click on the Domain on the Left-Hand navigation

- On the Right-Hand Navigation click on Properties

- Scroll to the bottom of the Properties page and edit the Custom Properties

Under Properties , go to Custom Properties > Edit

- Specify the parameter name MasterDBRefreshInterval and the value in seconds to 30

Example

MasterDBRefreshInterval=30 default value is 8

Please set following resilience properties in Admin Console:

Domain Properties:

Resilience Timeout: 120

Limit on Resilience Timeouts: 900

Repository Service:

DatabaseConnectionTimeOut: 360

Heartbeat Interval: 180

Resilience Timeout: 360

Limit on Resilience Timeouts: 360

Integration Service:

Resilience Timeout: 900

Limit on Resilience Timeouts: 900

To increase these properties please set the following values. These changes require node to restart to be effective.

1. infa.masterUpdateTimeInterval and infa.updateMasterConnectTimeout need to be added in $INFA_HOME/tomcat/bin/infaservice.sh by appending to the INFA_JAVA_OPTS variable. Please take a backup of infaservice.sh file before editing it. Make these changes on all gateway nodes.

Example

setenv INFA_JAVA_OPTS="${INFA_JAVA_OPTS} -Dinfa.masterUpdateTimeInterval=45000 -Dinfa.updateMasterConnectTimeout

=20"

INFA_JAVA_OPTS="-Xmx1024m ${INFA_JAVA_OPTS} -XX:GCTimeRatio=9 -XX:MaxPermSize=128m -Dinfa.masterUpdateTimeInterval=25000 -Dinfa.updateMasterConnectTimeout=10"

NOTE:-The modification for INFA_JAVA_OPTS is only for masterUpdateTimeInterval & updateMasterConnectTimeout other parameters should be as it is.

11.Make the same modification on all nodes.

12.Restart Informatica services on each node.

Please refer this kb for more information about MasterUpdateTimeInterval --

﷟HYPERLINK "https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/152051?language=en_US"FAQ: What is MasterUpdateTimeInterval and how to check if the value has been updated?

How to fix the ODBC 32bit oracle data source connections issue

First need to check the client installed or not if not need to download and install the odbc client.

url:https://www.oracle.com/database/technologies/oracle19c-windows-downloads.html

> Download the NT_193000_client.zip, extract and run the setup and choose the client install.

set the ORACLE_HOME for the client installation.

create a new folder for TNS ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/tnsnames.ors

now set the env for ORACLE_HOME, TNS_ADMIN, and add PATH for ORACLE_HOME/bin.

Check the Registry Editor for

Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\ODBC\ODBCINST.INI\ODBC Drivers

in the above dir and see if the oracle_client_32bit drivers are installed on the right side.

Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\ODBC\ODBCINST.INI\

in the above dir look for the drivers folder and check the driver and setup are pointing to the right .dll files.

example: Driver: C:\oracle_193000_client_32bit\SQORA32.DLL

Setup: C:\oracle_193000_client_32bit\SQORAS32.DLL

Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\

look in the above location and open ORACLE folder and select the oracle_32bit_client and verify the right side for the below entries

ORACLE_GROUP_NAME

ORACLE_HOME

ORACLE_HOME_NAME

If there are already DSN (Data Source Name) added by the current user then need to check this Registry Entries.

Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\ODBC\ODBC.INI.

In the above DIR look for the below folders.

ODBC

ODBC Data Source (in this folder on the right verify that the driver created by user is there)

ODBC File DSN (verify the DSN base directory which is nothing but the driver name where the client drivers are installed)

Oracle_19c_32bit (this is the user created client drivers entry) > in this driver check for (Driver, DSN,ServiceName (for the oracle connection),UserID(schema id))

If Session failed with no error are less info in the wf logs like Execution failed, then need to update the ODBC.ini file with below parameters.

Update the LoginTimeout=300 (it is set to 15 now)

Update ConnectionRetryCount=5 (set to 0 now)

Update ConnectionRetryDelay=5 (set to 3 now)

SeedBeforeConnect=1

KeepAlive=1

FAQ: how does an ODBC DSN-less connection work in PowerCenter?

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000209927

Need to ask the following questions if the ODBC connection in source/target

Please share the following information to start the investigation:

1.Is this a new source/target connection?

2.Any activities performed on the database side?

3.Please share some failed session logs.

4.Please check the connection and share the output with us:

Navigate to $INFA_HOME/tools/debogtools/ssgodbc/linux64/

Run ./ssgodbc.linux64 -d DSN_in_odbc.ini -u DB_user_name -p DB_passwd [-v]

pages.title: RS - SQL Query
pages.content:
RS - SQL Query

Thursday, 26 October, 2023

12:58 AM

Below queries from your repository database.

Select * from OPB_SERVER_INFO;

select distinct SERVER_NAME, SERVER_ID from OPB_WFLOW_RUN;

Need work flow runtime details of workflow name, sessions names, sources, targets and mappings

Below is the provided queryto get workflow, session, mapping names, sources, targets, lookups tables names info used in a folder during runtime

SELECT SUBJ_NAME FOLDER,

W.TASK_NAME WORKFLOW,

I.INSTANCE_NAME"SESSION",M.MAPPING_NAME,

CASE S.WIDGET_TYPE

WHEN 1 THEN 'SOURCE'

WHEN 3 THEN 'SOURCE'

WHEN 11 THEN 'LOOKUP'

WHEN 2 THEN 'TARGET'

END AS OBJECT_TYPE,

LOCATION_NAME,

CASE LOCATION_TYPE

WHEN 1 THEN 'FILE'

WHEN 2 THEN 'TABLE'

END AS LOCATION_TYPE

FROM OPB_DTL_SWIDG_LOG D,

OPB_TASK W,

OPB_TASK_INST I,

OPB_SWIDGET_INST S,OPB_MAPPING M,

OPB_SUBJECT B

WHERE D.WORKFLOW_ID=W.TASK_ID

AND W.TASK_TYPE=71

AND W.IS_VISIBLE=1

AND D.TASK_INSTANCE_ID =I.INSTANCE_ID

AND I.TASK_TYPE=68

AND I.TASK_ID=S.SESSION_ID

AND D.SESSWIDG_INST_ID = S.SESS_WIDG_INST_ID

AND S.WIDGET_TYPEIN (1,2,3,11)

AND W.SUBJECT_ID=B.SUBJ_ID

AND S.MAPPING_ID=M.MAPPING_ID

AND M.IS_VISIBLE=1

AND LOCATION_TYPE IN (1,2)

More optimized query

SELECT

B.SUBJ_NAME AS FOLDER,

W.TASK_NAME AS WORKFLOW,

I.INSTANCE_NAME AS "SESSION",

M.MAPPING_NAME,

CASE S.WIDGET_TYPE

WHEN 1 THEN 'SOURCE'

WHEN 3 THEN 'SOURCE'

WHEN 11 THEN 'LOOKUP'

WHEN 2 THEN 'TARGET'

END AS OBJECT_TYPE,

LOCATION_NAME,

CASE LOCATION_TYPE

WHEN 1 THEN 'FILE'

WHEN 2 THEN 'TABLE'

END AS LOCATION_TYPE

FROM

OPB_DTL_SWIDG_LOG D

JOIN

OPB_TASK W ON D.WORKFLOW_ID = W.TASK_ID AND W.TASK_TYPE = 71 AND W.IS_VISIBLE = 1

JOIN

OPB_TASK_INST I ON D.TASK_INSTANCE_ID = I.INSTANCE_ID AND I.TASK_TYPE = 68

JOIN

OPB_SWIDGET_INST S ON I.TASK_ID = S.SESSION_ID AND D.SESSWIDG_INST_ID = S.SESS_WIDG_INST_ID AND S.WIDGET_TYPE IN (1, 2, 3, 11)

JOIN

OPB_MAPPING M ON S.MAPPING_ID = M.MAPPING_ID AND M.IS_VISIBLE = 1

JOIN

OPB_SUBJECT B ON W.SUBJECT_ID = B.SUBJ_ID

WHERE

LOCATION_TYPE IN (1, 2);

pages.title: Core File / Stack trace
pages.content:
Core File / Stack trace

Wednesday, 20 December, 2023

10:58 PM

1. By default, we generate the core files in the same directory where the corresponding executable is present.

For example, if node process is crashing, core file generates in tomcat/bin directory.

And, if PMDTM (or) PMSERVER (or) PMREPAGENT processes crash, core file generates in $INFA_HOME/server/bin directory.

2. Please check if you notice any service failure or session failures during that time.

3. Also, you can run strings command or pmstack command as mentioned by Smitha to find out which process is generating these core files.

4. You see few lines in the PMSTACK output which gives details about executable :

Core info :

-rw------- 1 sa-diq-d sa-diq-d 117415936 Mar 3 07:58 /apps/Informatica/v101/server/bin/core.33426

Cause and Analysis of Unix Core files

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/15627

Why core file is generated, and how to analyse it?

Cause and Analysis of Unix Core files - (INTERNAL KB)

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/15627

A core file is generated in UNIX when a fatal run-time error occurs in a program, leading to the termination of the UNIX process by the operating system. The core file serves as a snapshot of the program's memory at the time of the abnormal termination. This file is created to help analyze the cause of the unexpected termination. The reasons for generating a core file are typically linked to bad behavior by the program, such as attempts to execute illegal instructions, improper memory allocation, or writing to read-only memory. Additionally, UNIX may terminate a process and create a core file if the process exceeds its CPU time limit or surpasses its allocated memory limit, even in the absence of programming errors.

-Informatica generate the core files in the same directory where the corresponding executable is present.

-For example, if node process is crashing, core file generates in tomcat/bin directory.

-And, if PMDTM (or) PMSERVER (or) PMREPAGENT processes crash, core file generates in $INFA_HOME/server/bin directory.

-Please check if you notice any service failure or session failures during that time.

-------------------------

Go to the/tools/debugtools/pmstack/directory.

./pmstack-c/server/bin/core.12345

this will generate a trace file in the same directory, please collect the trace on multiple core files and send them to us.

pages.content:
HA / Grid

Wednesday, 3 January, 2024

11:43 PM

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/510424?language=en_US

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/18593?language=en_US

The coordination between nodes in a domain is handled by the master gateway node. It checks in very short intervals which of the registered nodes are up and running and updates its database tables accordingly.

In terms of a session running on a server grid in PowerCenter, there's a "master" PMDTM process running on one node (determined by the Integration Service). All other nodes which run a PMDTM for this particular session run these PMDTM processes as "slave" processes, meaning the "master" is responsible for coordinating all these slave PMDTM processes.

If at any time the master PMDTM fails (e.g. because the node crashes), then one of the other PMDTM processes will take over responsibility as the master PMDTM, and the whole game continues.

The Master PMDTM process is also responsible for defining the names of cache files and so on. These file names are then handed over to the slave PMDTM processes so that they can access the same cache files whenever necessary. That's one reason why for a server grid all infa_shared/ mounts must be in the same place.

When you configure PowerCenter to run on a grid, you specify the storage location for different types of session files, such as source files, log files, and cache files. To improve performance, store files in optimal locations. For example, store persistent cache files on a high-bandwidth shared file system. Different types of files have different storage requirements.

You can store files in the following types of locations:

Shared file systems. Store files on a shared file system to enable all Integration Service processes to access the same files. You can store files on low-bandwidth and high-bandwidth shared file systems.

Local. Store files on the local machine running the Integration Service process when the files do not have to be accessed by other Integration Service processes

﷟HYPERLINK "https://infa.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka0VM000000jEQDYA2/view"HOW TO: Enable Valgrind setup in Informatica server for CPU and Memory leak issues | Knowledge | Salesforce

HOW TO: Enable Valgrind setup in Informatica server for CPU and Memory leak issues

Solution

1. shutdown RS from admin console

2. from linux terminal go to"$INFA_HOME/server/bin"

3. change the name of pmrepagent binary to pmrepagent_orig

$ mv pmrepagent pmrepagent_orig

4. create a file name as pmrepagent and add below content .

$ vi pmrepagent

put below exec command in the pmrepagent file

exec /usr/bin/valgrind --tool=memcheck --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=all --log-file=/path/valgrind.log $INFA_HOME/server/bin/pmrepagent_orig $@

$ give execute permission

note : make sure"/usr/bin/valgrind"installed

note2 : --log-file=/path/valgrind.log ( please provide a path & file name where sufficiet space availabe )

note3 : we should choose a local drive path for --log-file

5. check the RS and its log level should"INFO"

6. start the RS

7. validate if log file created also validate if valgrind is running

===================================================

=give usual load and replicate the issue ======

=In your case check if memory growth for RS reaches to 8-10GB ====

=Once partial issue replicated then shutdown the RS manually from admin console with"complete"

====================================================

8. Now revert back the changes :

$ cd $INFA_HOME/server/bin

$ mv pmrepagen pmrepagen_script

$ mv pmrepagent_orig pmrepagent

$ start the RS

9. Now provide the valgrind log & RS log & IS log for investigation .

VERY IMP INFO :Valgrind will reduce the performance so test it in your lower environment first and provide the report to us . once we validated then deploy it in production .

Power Exchange connector for PostgreSQL require an additional License, and you need contact your Sales Representative for further questions regarding licensing and cost.

Answers to other questions asked during the meeting.

The official ODBC Support Statement you will find an attachment in the files section from the below document:

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/DOC-18899?language=en_US

What is the purpose of postgres_Plugin.xml located in $INFA_HOME/server/bin/Plugin, and they have registered the repository with this plugin?

The plugin adds the specific options for using the PowerExchange for PostGreSQL functionality in the clients (import objects, PWX connection type, etc.).

Reference: https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/powerexchange-adapters-for-powercenter/10-5-6/powerexchange-for-postgresql-user-guide-for-powercenter/powerexchange-for-postgresql-configuration/registering-the-plug-in.html

Does PowerExchange for PostgreSQL support Kerberos authentication?

Kerberos authentication is not supported by the Native PostgreSQL connector, a feature request(OCON-30797).

Reference:﷟HYPERLINK "https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000216065?language=en_US"FAQ: Is Kerberos authentication supported by the Native PostgreSQL connector? (informatica.com)

Does PowerExchange for PostgreSQL support TLS/SSL?

Yes.

Refer to documentation: https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/powerexchange-adapters-for-powercenter/10-5-6/powerexchange-for-postgresql-user-guide-for-powercenter/postgresql-sessions/powerexchange-for-postgresql-connection-properties.html

Why is an additional license required for the PostgreSQL plugin when it is already available in $INFA_HOME/server/bin/Plugin?

A license is required as this is an additional option. The plugin is there since an additional installation is not required.

If we use the native PowerExchange connector for PostgreSQL, will it utilize ODBC or JDBC?

The native PowerExchange connector for PostgreSQL uses JDBC. The JDBC library is included with the connector, so no additional client or library is required for connectivity.

pages.content:
PowerCenter Upgrade

Thursday, 18 January, 2024

03:39 AM

FAQ: What are different ways to upgrade Informatica application/Domain?

https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000079832?language=en_US

These prerequisites are provided to ensure that in case any issues arise during the upgrade process, we have the necessary details to easily revert the changes.

Perform a complete backup of the domain using the 'infasetup backupDomain' command.

Backup the Repository services (PCRS, MRS) from the Admin Console.

Create backups of specific configuration files such as nodemeta.xml, keystore, truststore, sitekey, and ODBC.ini, as well as the bash_profile.

Save a backup copy of the metadata manager properties file, known as the 'tnsname.ora' file.

Backup metadata for both the domain and Repository services tables from the database side.

NOTE: Before upgrading, please check the compatibility of OS, DB version and its type.

Upgrade from 10.5 to 10.5.x - this is considered as hotfix not an full upgrade

Please refer to this document on applying the hotfix to upgrade from 10.5.1 to 10.5.6 - ﷟HYPERLINK "https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/powercenter/10-5-6/upgrading-from-version-10-4-0-and-later--10-5-6-/apply-the-hotfix/applying-the-services-hotfix/apply-the-services-hotfix-in-graphical-mode.html"Apply the services hotfix in graphical mode (informatica.com)

Note: Before applying the Hotfix make sure to take the below backups and keep them outside of the $INFA_HOME directory as the upgrade will remove and recreate few folders during the upgrade process.

-Backup Domain and the Repository services and also other services like MRS if exists.

-Backup nodemeta.xml from $INFA_HOME/isp/config

-Backup sitekey from $INFA_HOME/isp/config/keys

-Backup odbc.ini from $INFA_HOME/ODBC7.1

The commands and other related information about backups refer to this KB: ﷟HYPERLINK "https://knowledge.informatica.com/s/article/000079832?language=en_US"FAQ: What are different ways to upgrade Informatica application/Domain?

pages.title: TNS issue
pages.content:
TNS issue

Wednesday, 11 June, 2025

12:15 AM

ORA-12154: TNS:could not resolve the connect identifier specified

To resolve the error, follow the steps below to ensure Oracle connectivity:

Log on to the PowerCenter host machine and connect to the Oracle database using SQL*Plus (do not use other tools, such as Toad).

Log in to the Oracle database using SQL*Plus with exactly same username/password and connection string of Powercenter Repository.

Ensure that the TNS_ADMIN environment variable is set to the correct TNSNAMES.ORA file.

Check if the tnsname.ora file has full permission

Verify that the service name exists in the TNSNAMES.ORA files (Add it if necessary).

Syntax:

$ sqlplus /@connection_string

If you are not able to connect, then check with DBA and ensure that the client configuration and everything is correct. Once confirmed restart Informatica services and let us know your observation.

Note:

Please make sure the connection string [abc] is exactly the same as what you have given on the TNSNAMES.ORA files.

Please check and keep us posted on your observation.

From

pages.title: Yellowbrick
pages.content:
Yellowbrick

Thursday, April 25, 2024

5:11 AM

In the past, Informatica used to have a partner relationship with Yellowbrick(YB) company. YB used their ODBC driver and built a PWX connector, that Informatica packaged and released with PowerCenter installation. Customers used this YB PWX connector to import YB source/targets

But that relationship does not exist anymore with YB company. So, to import YB source and targets, Customer should use any level 2 or level 3 certified ODBC driver from the market and use that ODBC driver to import YB source and targets into repository

SO, the existing source/targets created with the PWX for YB connector should be replaced with YB ODBC driver

But if you want to continue with the source/targets created previously by PWX for YB connector and does not want to change to ODBC source/targets, then you should contact Yellowbrick support for further assistance.

Next Step:

Step 1: Install any level 2 or level 3 certified ODBC driver from the market and use that ODBC driver. you should acquire this driver directly from YB or any vendor in the market

Step 2: If they want to use or continue with PWX for YB connector, then you need to contact YB support for assistance. But, Informatica recommends changing the source/targets used with PWX for YB to generic ODBC source/targets, as mentioned in step 1

pages.title: Old Database Related Questions
pages.content:
Old Database Related Questions

Tuesday, 26 September, 2023

03:35 AM

This is Birsen Gokcek, Support Manager. I apologize your question wasn't answered sooner. Case 04534018

>Can you help us the reason/logic behind stopping the support for sql 2014 starting from 10.5.x?

There is no specific reason for stopping support for SQL Server 2014. We can certify and support a number of database versions. At some point we need to decommission support for older database versions.

>Any specific functionalities that is not compatible between sql 2014 and informatica 10.5.x?

No there is no specific functionality that is not supported. Basically product is not tested and verified with SQL Server 2014 version. Application may and probably will still work. But since it is not certified/supported, if you run into any issues you will not receive support.

We strongly recommend using supported version of databases.

Customers who wish to upgrade PowerCenter to 10.5.7, but also have PowerExchange CDC will have compatibility questions. PowerExchange 10.5.7 is planned to be release on mid 2025. Ideally, we require customer to be on the same version of PC and PWX. However, because of the delay with PWX, R&D did further investigation. The result of this investigation is as follows:

PowerCenter 10.5.7 can be combined with PowerExchange 10.5.6 if following items are true.

PowerExchange Service is ‘not’ configured in Admin Console

pages.title: SMTP / Email Task
pages.content:
SMTP / Email Task

Thursday, June 27, 2024

1:52 AM

In PowerCenter, both SMTP and sendmail are used for sending emails, but they are used in different contexts and have different configurations.

SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) is used by the Mail Transfer Agent (MTA) to deliver email to the recipient mail server. The protocol can only be used to send emails, not to receive them. In PowerCenter, you can configure SMTP settings for the domain. The outgoing mail server uses the SMTP settings to send alerts and scorecard notifications. You can find detailed instructions on how to configure SMTP settings in PowerCenter in the document titled "SMTP Configuration" [here]( ﷟HYPERLINK "https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/common-content-for-data-integration/10-5-1/administrator-guide/domain-management/domain-properties/smtp-configuration.html)."https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/common-content-for-data-integration/10-5-1/administrator-guide/domain-management/domain-properties/smtp-configuration.html). Also, the Integration Service can send email using SMTP if authentication is disabled on the SMTP server.

On the other hand, sendmail is used by the PowerCenter Integration Service to send email on Linux. Before you configure email in a session or workflow, you need to verify that the sendmail tool is accessible on the Linux machines. You can find detailed instructions on how to verify sendmail on Linux in the document titled "Verifying sendmail on Linux" [here]( ﷟HYPERLINK "https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/powercenter/10-5/workflow-basics-guide/sending-email/configuring-email-on-unix/verifying-sendmail-on-linux.html)."https://docs.informatica.com/data-integration/powercenter/10-5/workflow-basics-guide/sending-email/configuring-email-on-unix/verifying-sendmail-on-linux.html).

Please note that the choice between SMTP and sendmail often depends on your specific requirements and the system environment.
